title: Surface disinfectants for emerging viral pathogens - Canada.ca
content:
## Surface disinfectants for emerging viral pathogens
- What are emerging viral pathogens
- How a disinfectant works against EVPs
- List of surface disinfectants for EVPs
- For more information

## What are emerging viral pathogens
Emerging viral pathogens (EVP) are viruses that have either:
- just appeared in a population or
- been around but are quickly increasing in numbers or geographic range
When an EVP first appears, there is a period of uncertainty whereby the length of time the virus can survive on surfaces and the contribution of surface transmission to overall disease transmission may not be known. Furthermore, few, if any authorized surface disinfectants will have been tested and approved for use against this virus during that time.
How effective a surface disinfectant is depends on 2 things:
- the type of virus and
- how a disinfectant product is used
There are 2 types of viruses:
Enveloped viruses have fat-based envelopes. Both the SARS-CoV-2 (the virus that causes COVID-19) and mpox viruses are enveloped viruses.
Non-enveloped viruses don't have fat-based envelopes (for example, norovirus and poliovirus).
Non-enveloped viruses are more resistant than enveloped viruses.

## How a disinfectant works against EVPs
Surface disinfectants target fat-based envelopes. The act of targeting inactivates the virus.
Because non-enveloped viruses lack fat-based envelopes, they are more resistant to surface disinfectants.
Surface disinfectants that are effective against non-enveloped viruses will probably also be effective against enveloped viruses.
When an EVP emerges, Health Canada allows disinfectant manufacturers to reference existing information that we have on file for efficacy claims against non-enveloped viruses. The manufacturers can also let the public know how effective they believe their surface disinfectant is against the emerging virus.
It is important that these products are used following the directions for use on the product label.

## List of surface disinfectants for EVPs
The "List of surface disinfectants for emerging viral pathogens" contains disinfectants approved by Health Canada. This means they are supported by evidence against an eligible non-enveloped virus.
As for any drug product approved by Health Canada for sale in Canada, these disinfectant products have a drug identification number (DIN). However, the EVP list is not comprehensive. It does not contain all disinfectants that may be appropriate for use in public, institutional or household spaces. Health Canada will update this list with additional products as evidence is provided from industry applicants.
Note that a product appearing on this list does not mean Health Canada has endorsed it.
How to determine if your disinfectant product is on Health Canada's List of surface disinfectants for emerging viral pathogens
- Locate the DIN on the disinfectant product label
- Search the DIN on the List of surface disinfectants for emerging viral pathogens
